name: Kahun Danda
description: A ridge viewpoint east of Pokhara featuring the Kahun Dharahara tower. Offers panoramic views of the Annapurna range and is quieter than Sarangkot.

name: Dhampus
description: A traditional Gurung village and ridge viewpoint north of Pokhara. Gateway to regional treks with views of Annapurna and Dhaulagiri.

name: Astam
description: An eco-village focused on sustainability and organic farming. Offers 360-degree views of the Annapurna range from a peaceful ridge setting.
amenities: Eco-tourism|360 Degree View|Organic Food|Hiking|Village Walk|Local Tea

name: Hemjakot
description: A community homestay village on the foothills of Mount Machhapuchhare. Features traditional stone architecture and authentic rural lifestyle.

name: Lwang Ghalel
description: A village known for its large organic tea gardens and successful community homestays. Center for canyoning and cultural shows.

name: Sikles
description: One of the largest traditional Gurung settlements in Kaski. Features stone-roofed houses and serves as the gateway to Kapuche Glacier Lake.

name: Mattikhana
description: A tranquil hill station southwest of Pokhara on the Syangja border. Offers views of the valley and multiple surrounding districts.

name: Naudanda
description: A village on the ridge path towards Sarangkot. Famous for birdwatching and views of the Annapurna range and Phewa Lake.

name: Panchase
description: A high-altitude ridge and protected biodiversity area west of Pokhara. A significant spiritual site with five distinct peaks.

name: Australian Camp
description: A hilltop settlement at 2,100 meters offering expansive Himalayan views. A popular resting point for hikers in the Annapurna region.

name: Pothana
description: A small village within the Annapurna Conservation Area surrounded by forests. Popular rest stop on the Mardi Himal trek.

name: Tangting
description: A pristine Gurung village northeast of Pokhara with cobblestoned paths. Maintains its traditional culture away from mainstream tourism.

name: Begnas Lake
description: The second largest lake in the valley, known for pure water and a peaceful environment. Offers boating and nature walks.

name: Rupa Lake
description: A tranquil freshwater lake separated from Begnas by a ridge. Prime location for birdwatching and wetland observation.

name: Dipang Lake
description: A small, isolated lake in Lekhnath known for high biodiversity. A quiet destination for nature lovers away from crowds.

name: Khaste Lake
description: A wetland lake serving as a vital bird habitat. Managed by local communities for eco-tourism and conservation.

name: Maidi Lake
description: The smallest lake in the valley cluster, characterized by marshy wetlands. An offbeat site for ecosystem observation.

name: Sarangkot
description: A famous hilltop overlooking Pokhara city. Center for paragliding and accessible sunrise views over the Himalayas.

name: Pumdikot
description: A hilltop site southwest of Pokhara featuring a massive Shiva statue. Offers aerial views of the lake and city.

name: Chapakot
description: A rural village on the western valley edge known for terraced farms. Features trails connecting the hills to Phewa Lake.

name: Bhadaure
description: A Gurung village on the edge of the Panchase forest. Known for traditional architecture and mountain agriculture.

name: Kaskikot
description: A historic hilltop village once serving as a royal seat. Features fort ruins and expansive mountain views.

name: Ghachok
description: A mountain village 20km from Pokhara surrounded by the Annapurna Massif. Known for waterfalls and peaceful atmosphere.

name: Armala Kot
description: A lesser-known ridge viewpoint northeast of Pokhara. Offers views of terraced hillsides and the northern range.

name: Thulakot
description: A ridge viewpoint overlooking both Begnas and Rupa Lakes. Offers a dual perspective of lakes and Himalayas.

name: Kalabang
description: A village on the southern shores of Phewa Lake. Known for its community homestays and forest hiking trails.

name: Kristi Nachnechaur
description: A hill station south of the city core. Popular for local day trips and panoramic views of the valley floor.

name: Sundari Danda
description: A ridge village situated between the two major eastern lakes. A center for organic farming and local agriculture.

name: Bhalam
description: A settlement on the northern slopes of the valley. Emerging destination for cultural tourism and quiet escapes.

name: Maujakot
description: A remote Gurung village on the Royal Trek route. Features traditional mid-hill farming and culture.

name: Phosingkot
description: A quiet hilltop southwest of Pokhara. Ideal for sunrise and sunset viewing away from tourist crowds.

name: Dhikki Danda
description: A small ridge overlooking the eastern valley. Visited for its peaceful atmosphere and mountain vistas.

name: Tiger Hill
description: A high point overlooking the Bijayapur river. Offers distinct perspectives of the Machhapuchhre peak.

name: Karuwa
description: A tranquil mountain village known for warm hospitality. Features pristine landscapes and traditional rural life.

name: World Peace Pagoda (Anadu Danda)
description: The ridge across Phewa Lake housing the World Peace Pagoda. Offers a meditative atmosphere and scenic vistas of the city and Himalayas.

name: Methlang
description: A western hilltop offering views of the Sarangkot ridge. A quiet alternative for city and mountain observation.

name: Lovely Hill
description: A popular viewpoint near the city center. Provides a full overview of the Pokhara urban area and surrounding hills.

name: Taprang
description: A small village in the Madi River valley. A refreshing rest stop on rural trails towards the high hills.

name: Chipli
description: A traditional settlement in the high hills of Kaski. Surrounded by dense forests and wildflowers.

name: Khilang
description: A Gurung settlement near the northern mountains. Known for its traditional customs and mid-hill farming.

name: Saimarang
description: A rural village in eastern Kaski. Provides views of the Lamjung Himal and regional hill traditions.

name: Majhthana
description: A hilly settlement southeast of Pokhara. Represents the semi-rural character of the valley's surrounding hills.

name: Thumakodada
description: A hilltop village overlooking the river valley. Offbeat destination for natural vistas and solitude.

name: Nirmalpokhari
description: A ridge village south of Pokhara known for orange orchards. Offers mountain views across the valley.

name: Bharatpokhari
description: A hilly village representing the transition to rural life. Known for agriculture and local hill paths.

name: Puranchaur
description: A village on the valley floor northeast of city. Hub for regional agricultural research and foothill trails.

name: Lahachok
description: A multi-ethnic village in the Mardi valley. Known for traditional farming and its role as a trailhead for hill treks.
